name: Mushroom Tempeh
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs mushrooms (mix of buttons or field and portobello)
salt and freshly cracked pepper to season
1/2 lb oblong (or rectangular) loaf of wholemeal bread, unsliced
recipeInstructions:
The Crou^tes:
1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F.
2. Cut the bread into four equal slices.
3. Make a well in the center of each slice and press down on the sides and bottom to create an open-topped casing with sides 1/2 - 1/4 inch thick.
4. Place the crou^tes on a baking sheet and lightly toast for 5-10 minutes or reheat at 350 degrees F for several minutes before serving.
Tempeh aux Champignons:
1. In a 3-quart saucepan, bring 4 cups vegetable stock, bay leaf, and peppercorns to a rolling boil.
2. Add the tempeh directly from the freezer to the stock and reduce heat. Simmer, turning occasionally for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside, covered.
3. While the tempeh is simmering, slice the onion into thin rings. Soften the sun-dried tomatoes in boiling water in a small bowl.
4. In a medium-sized or large non-stick skillet, braise/deglaze onions using temper cooking liquid as needed. Coarsely puree the roasted garlic and mince rosemary.
5. Wash and trim mushrooms; cut large buttons into halves or quarters and portobellos into 1/2-inch strips.
6. Toast flour in a large non-stick skillet until it begins to turn slightly toasted and darker, then pour into a bowl to cool.
7. Warm 2 1/2 cups of vegetable stock in a saucepan over medium heat until it steams. Mix the cooled flour with remaining stock and whisk into the saucepan while cooking at medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens.
8. Reduce heat to very low and add rosemary, garlic, tomatoes, and onions, continuing to cook the sauce as other preparations are made.
9. Remove tempeh from its cooking liquid and cut into 1/2-inch dice; season with salt and pepper (at least 1/2 tsp of pepper). Pour mushrooms into the sauce, stir in diced tempeh, and finish with remaining sherry.
Serve:
1. Place a warm crou^te in the center of each serving plate.
2. Fill with about one-fourth of the Tempeh aux Champignons.
3. Serve immediately.
